title: Select by Stable Scope and Relationship
summary: Build and verify css type class id selectors in survey component selector architecture through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css type class id selectors from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css type class id selectors without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

steps.title: 03 · Read the survey component selector architecture case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 3: Which claim preserves css type class id selectors when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: survey component selector architecture.
steps.why: Select by Stable Scope and Relationship makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 3 in survey component selector architecture.

content.text: Type selectors address element kinds, classes represent reusable component or state hooks, IDs target unique document identities, attributes match explicit data, and combinators express relationships. Good selectors use the least brittle scope that communicates ownership. More specificity is not inherently safer; it can make component states and overrides difficult to reason about. Apply it to survey component selector architecture.
